texto: Shame, as an emotion, has a core meaning, in relating individuals to wider social groups and norms -- real or imagined.
explicacao: Citação de Peter N. Stearns sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Shame, as an emotion, has a core meaning, in relating individuals to wider social groups and norm…” articula reflexao, history, shame em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como shame e sociology.

seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Peter N. Stearns sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Shame, as an emotion, has a core meaning, in relating individuals to wider social groups and norm…

frase_original: Shame, as an emotion, has a core meaning, in relating individuals to wider social groups and norms -- real or imagined.

texto: Guilty people apologize and also take steps to avoid repetition. Shame, in contrast, is a more global emotion, which can emerge in response to the same kind of wrong act and violation of standards. It may develop earlier in life than guilt-- guilt requires more cognitive sorting capacity-- but above all it emphasizes self-abasement. It is the self that is at fault, not the commission of the act. This creates greater pain and intensity than guilt. A shamed person feels very bad indeed-- but also makes it more difficult to escape.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Peter N. Stearns sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Guilty people apologize and also take steps to avoid repetition. Shame, in contrast, is a more gl…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, guilt, shame, útil em momentos de shame e sociology.
